pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh TARGET_FILE and verifier.py 'is_target_filename' check.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which generates this file in ~/Documents/SweetHome3D/.
Confirmed by summing the max points from C1 (20) + C2 (15) + C3 (25) + C4 (25) + C5 (15) in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: Confirmed that the task data is derived from a real Sweet Home 3D official gallery sample ('SweetHome3DExample7.sh3d'). The setup script unzips this real file and programmatically removes all furniture, rooms, labels, and dimensions to create a clean 'bike_shop_starter.sh3d' shell. All metadata values (expected_result_file, starter_file, pass_threshold of 70, and total_points of 100) exactly match the export and evaluation scripts. However, a minor logical flaw exists in the verifier: while the prompt requires at least two sinks (one in the workshop and one in the restroom), the verifier only checks for a single global sink (sink_count >= 1).
